patch:
diff --git a/src/filter_core/filter.c b/src/filter_core/filter.c
index c052ca7..910c653 100644
--- a/src/filter_core/filter.c
+++ b/src/filter_core/filter.c
@@ -35,7 +35,13 @@ void gf_void_del(void *p)
 void gf_filterpacket_del(void *p)
 {
 	GF_FilterPacket *pck=(GF_FilterPacket *)p;
-	if (pck->data) gf_free(pck->data);
+	/*
+	 * Data buffers stored in the allocation reservoir may be shared/swapped between
+	 * packet structures and can be reallocated/freed elsewhere (see gf_filter_pck_expand).
+	 * Freeing the data pointer here can therefore lead to a double-free. The packet
+	 * structure itself still needs to be freed. The underlying data buffers will be
+	 * freed by their final owners when appropriate.
+	 */
 	gf_free(p);
 }

Credits:
---------

This issue was reported by Sergej Schumilo, Daniel Teuchert and Cornelius Aschermann (https://hackerone.com/schumilo).

## Impact

This results in negative integers being passed to `mrb_str_resize`, which will set the string length without further checks. This can potentially result in a oversized string that allows to access arbitrary memory.


Comments:
Comment by clayton-shopify on 2018-07-19 18:37:11+00:00:
The extra check added in 695f29cd604787f43be1af16e38d13610bf8312b prevents the crash, but undefined behaviour is still invoked. This can be seen using UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer in clang:
```
$ echo "' %8646911284551352320d'%1" | bin/mruby

bug_description: The CHECK macro in mrbgems/mruby-sprintf/src/sprintf.c in mruby 1.4.1 contains a signed integer overflow, possibly leading to out-of-bounds memory access because the mrb_str_resize function in string.c does not check for a negative length.
patch:
diff --git a/mrbgems/mruby-sprintf/src/sprintf.c b/mrbgems/mruby-sprintf/src/sprintf.c
index 7eea1a1..eca3959 100644
--- a/mrbgems/mruby-sprintf/src/sprintf.c
+++ b/mrbgems/mruby-sprintf/src/sprintf.c
@@ -121,8 +121,8 @@ mrb_fix2binstr(mrb_state *mrb, mrb_value x, int base)
 #define CHECK(l) do {\
 /*  int cr = ENC_CODERANGE(result);*/\
   while ((l) >= bsiz - blen) {\
-    bsiz*=2;\
-    if (bsiz < 0) mrb_raise(mrb, E_ARGUMENT_ERROR, "too big specifier"); \
+    if (bsiz > (MRB_INT_MAX / 2)) mrb_raise(mrb, E_ARGUMENT_ERROR, "too big specifier"); \
+    bsiz *= 2;\
   }\
   mrb_str_resize(mrb, result, bsiz);\
 /*  ENC_CODERANGE_SET(result, cr);*/\
